Commitments And Performance Guarantees

Performance Guarantees – Contracts that contingently require the guarantor to make payments to the guaranteed party based on another entity’s failure to perform under an obligating agreement. Projected pension expenses for 2013
Projected pension expense

Projected amount of pension benefit costs expected to be recognized during the next fiscal year for (1) defined benefit plans and (2) defined contribution plans. For defined benefit plans, pension expense includes the following components: service cost, interest cost, expected return on plan assets, gain (loss) on plan assets, prior service cost or credit, transition asset or obligation, and gain (loss) due to settlements or curtailments. For defined contribution plans, the pension expense generally equals the firm's contribution to employees' accounts (if the firm contributes) during the period.

Segment Operating Income
Segment Operating Income (Loss)

Segment operating income is the measure of profit and loss that the Company’s chief operating decision maker uses to evaluate the financial performance of the business and as the basis for performance recviews, compensation and resource allocation. For these reasons, the Company believes that Segment operating income represents the most relevant measure of segment profit and loss. The Company may exclude certain charges or gains from Operating income to arrive at a Segment operating income that is a more meaningful measure of profit and loss upon within to base its operating decisions.

Total Asset For Probable Asbestos Related Insurance Recoveries

Receipt of a special cash payment

In connection with the Transaction, Ingersoll-Rand Services Company, an affiliate of Ingersoll Rand Industrial, borrowed an aggregate principal amount of $1.9 billion under a senior secured first lien term loan facility (Term Loan), the proceeds of which were used to make a special cash payment of $1.9 billion to a subsidiary of the Company.

Total Asbestos Related Liabilities

Net periodic benefit cost
DefinedBenefitPlanNetPeriodicBenefitCostBeforeCurtailmentSettlementGainsLosses

The total amount of net periodic benefit cost for defined benefit plans for the period before curtailment or settlement gains or losses. Postretirement benefit cost
Postretirement Cost Projected

Projected amount of other (such as medical, dental and life insurance) postretirement benefit costs expected to be recognized during the next fiscal year for (1) defined benefit plans (periodic benefit costs include the following components: service cost, interest cost, expected return on plan assets, gain (loss) on assets, prior service cost or credit, transition asset or obligation, and gain (loss) due to settlements or curtailments) and for (2) defined contribution plans (to the extent that a plan's defined contributions to an individual's account are to be made for periods in which that individual renders services, the net cost for a period is the contribution called for in that period; if a plan calls for contributions for periods after an individual retires or terminates, the estimated cost is accrued during the employee's service period).
